titleStmt.title: SII 23.315: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 315. (A.R. No. 315 of 1907.) Tiruviśalūr, Kumbhakonam Taluk, Tanjavur District Śivayōganātha temple—on the same wall Parāntaka I: year 23: 929-30 A.D.

summary.p: This records a gift of 190 sheep by Pūrvadēva-Aḍigaḷ, for burning two perpetual lamps in the temple of Perumāṉaḍigaḷ at Tiruviśalūr in Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam a dēvadāna and brahmādēya on the northern bank (of the Kāvēri) and provision for three more lights, one before Āditya-Piḍārar, (Sun-God), one before Kūtta-Perumāṉ (Naṭarāja) and one in the Tirukkāvaṇam hall. The donor is referred to as the mother of the king (Rājakēsarivarman) in No. 333 of 1907 assigned to Parāntaka II Sundara-Chōḷa. She must have been the consort of prince Ariñjaya. (Vide S.I.I. Vol. XIII, No. 271).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . matiraikoṇṭa kopparakesariparmaṟkku yāṇṭu 2 10 3Āvatu vaṭakarait tevatāṉa brahmateyam Amaninārāyaṇaccaturvedimaṅ
kalattu tiruvicalūrp perumāṉaṭikaḷukku pūrvadeva Aṭikaḷ niccal Erippa tarkku vaiytta viḷakku Iraṇṭu Āṭu nūṟṟutto
ṇṇūṟu Ātitta piṭārarkku Ivarey pakal viḷakkum kūttaperumā ṉukku pakal viḷakkum tirukāvaṇattukkum Āka tiruviḷakkeṇṇai
muḻākkey
Āḻākku ..
Digital edition of SII 23.315 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
